title: Default constructor doesn't initialize asymAlgAbility array
problem: The default constructor no longer initializes the asymAlgAbility array to a 2-element array. This will cause NullPointerException when getAsymAlgAbility() is called on a default-constructed object, or when setAsymAlgAbility() checks for null but other code expects a non-null array.

title: setC() validation depends on call order of setL()
problem: The validation `this.l > c.length` in setC() depends on l being set BEFORE c. If a caller calls setC() first, then setL() with a larger value, the object will be in an invalid state. The validation is ineffective.

title: setCtM() validation has call order dependency
problem: Similar to ECCCipher issue - validation `this.l1 > ctM.length` depends on l1 being set before ctM. Default l1 is 0, so validation passes even for invalid arrays.

title: setSigM() validation has call order dependency
problem: Same validation order issue - depends on l being set before sigM.

title: Constructor doesn't validate asymAlgAbility length
problem: The constructor checks for null but doesn't validate that asymAlgAbility has exactly 2 elements as documented. This can cause ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException or incorrect behavior when the array is used.

title: Constructor accepts null for prime and pexp arrays
problem: The constructor no longer validates that prime and pexp arrays are non-null or have exactly 2 elements. This can cause NullPointerException when these fields are accessed.

title: setPrime() and setPexp() accept null or invalid arrays
problem: The setters no longer validate null or array dimensions, allowing invalid state.

title: setX() and setY() don't validate array bounds
problem: Setters don't validate that coordinate arrays don't exceed ECC_MAX_LEN (64 bytes for 512-bit keys), allowing invalid state.

title: setK() doesn't validate array bounds
problem: Setter doesn't validate that K array doesn't exceed ECC_MAX_LEN (64 bytes).

title: setR() and setS() don't validate array bounds
problem: Setters don't validate that signature value arrays don't exceed ECC_MAX_LEN (64 bytes).

title: setX(), setY(), setM() don't validate array bounds
problem: Setters don't validate that coordinate/hash arrays don't exceed expected lengths.

title: setM() and setE() don't validate array bounds
problem: Setters don't validate that modulus/exponent arrays don't exceed RSA_MAX_LEN (512 bytes).

title: bytesToHex() outputs entire array without truncation
problem: For large arrays (e.g., RSA 512-byte keys), the toString() method will generate very long hex strings (1024+ hex characters). This was previously truncated for readability.

title: RSA array length check throws exception but documentation unclear
problem: The code throws an exception if array exceeds RSAref_MAX_LEN (512), but the Java layer constructors no longer enforce this limit. Users may create valid Java objects that fail at JNI boundary with unclear error message.

title: setM(), setE(), setD() don't validate array bounds
problem: Setters don't validate that byte arrays don't exceed RSA_MAX_LEN (512 bytes), allowing invalid state that will fail at JNI boundary.

title: setCoef() doesn't validate array bounds
problem: Setter doesn't validate that coef array doesn't exceed RSA_MAX_PLEN (256 bytes).
